title: What LLMs and Biology Have in Common | Max Agency
description_preview:
Software engineers are trained to think deterministically — write code, predict the output, debug the gap. But that mindset breaks down with LLMs. And according to Nicholas Larus-Stone, the people who are best at working with them are starting to look a lot more like biologists than engineers.

The analogy he draws is sharp: biologists have spent over a century probing systems they don't fully understand, running experiments, watching how things respond, and building knowledge iteratively. Inter

title: Observing And Testing CX Agents | Interrupt 26
description_preview:
At Interrupt, the agent conference by LangChain, Carlos Pereira from Cisco's Customer Experience team showed how you close the loop between production feedback and code at 16 million customer interactions per year.

The core problem with scaling agents: once adoption climbs, feedback volume outpaces your team. Every thumbs down, every confused user, every low-confidence routing decision is a signal. If you treat it as noise, or if your team becomes the bottleneck processing it, adoption drops. T

title: Debug web apps with browser use in Codex
description_preview:
Codex can now use the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) in Browser Use to inspect console logs, runtime errors, local storage, applied styling, network traffic, and performance profiles while working on web applications.

In this demo, Codex investigates a slow chat app by profiling interactions and inspecting network requests, then fixes the issues and supports the changes with clear measurements.

description_preview:

description_preview:
